Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We are given a sequence of events without explicit case IDs We need to group events that belong to the same patient journey case based on timestamp order and proximity activity logical sequence department where the event occurs patient attributes age and gender these should be consistent for a given case

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We see that 45 M appears in events 1 4 7 12 15 62 F appears in events 2 5 8 10 11 14 17 28 M appears in events 3 6 9 13 16 18 So we have three distinct patient profiles

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore we can group by age and gender But note what if two different patients have the same age and gender It is possible but in this dataset we have only three distinct profiles and each profile appears in multiple events that seem to form a coherent journey

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Now we must check the logical sequence and timing for each group

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Group 1 45 M events 1 4 7 12 15 Register patient ER Initial triage ER X ray imaging Radiology Doctor consultation ER Patient discharged ER The sequence Register Triage X ray Doctor consultation Discharge This is a logical ER visit

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Group 2 62 F events 2 5 8 10 11 14 17 Register patient ER Initial triage ER Lab test requested ER Lab test completed Laboratory Doctor consultation ER Prescription created ER Patient discharged ER Sequence Register Triage Lab test requested Lab test completed Doctor consultation Prescription Discharge This is a logical ER visit

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Group 3 28 M events 3 6 9 13 16 18 Register patient Surgery Doctor consultation Surgery Surgery preparation Surgery Surgery performed Surgery Recovery room Surgery Patient discharged Surgery Sequence Register Doctor consultation Surgery preparation Surgery performed Recovery room Discharged This is a logical surgical case

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We must ensure that the events for a given group are in increasing time order Group 1 timestamps are increasing Group 2 timestamps are increasing Group 3 timestamps are increasing

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Also note that the activities for each group form a logical sequence

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: However note what if two patients have the same age and gender In this dataset we have three distinct age gender pairs and each pair forms a coherent journey Therefore we can safely assign by age and gender

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The data does not suggest that the same patient comes twice Also the timestamps are all within a short period so it is unlikely that the same patient would have two separate visits in such a short time without being discharged in between Therefore we assume each distinct age gender pair corresponds to one case

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The department for the registration for group 1 and 2 is ER and for group 3 is Surgery This is consistent with the rest of the events

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore the case groupings are Case 1 events 1 4 7 12 15 Case 2 events 2 5 8 10 11 14 17 Case 3 events 3 6 9 13 16 18

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Event 1 Case 1 Event 2 Case 2 Event 3 Case 3 Event 4 Case 1 Event 5 Case 2 Event 6 Case 3 Event 7 Case 1 Event 8 Case 2 Event 9 Case 3 Event 10 Case 2 Event 11 Case 2 Event 12 Case 1 Event 13 Case 3 Event 14 Case 2 Event 15 Case 1 Event 16 Case 3 Event 17 Case 2 Event 18 Case 3
